dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.intro: LensFlow lets you build prescription flows for eyewear. Customers follow step-by-step forms to enter their prescription, select lenses, and place orders — all embedded in your Shopify storefront.

flows.title: Flows
flows.selectTypeHint: Select the flow type. This determines the default step order for your customers.
flows.prescriptionFirstDesc: Customer selects prescription type and submits prescription data first, then chooses lenses.
flows.prescriptionFirstFlow: Flow: Prescription Type → Submit Method → Prescription Form → Choose Lenses → Review
flows.lensFirstDesc: Customer selects lens type first, then provides prescription details.
flows.lensFirstFlow: Flow: Prescription Type → Choose Lenses → Submit Method → Prescription Form → Review
flows.name: Name
flows.intro: A Flow is a multi-step form that guides customers through prescription entry, lens selection, and order placement. Click a flow name to edit its steps, then publish it to make it live on your store.
flows.noFlowsHint: Create your first flow to start collecting prescription orders from customers on your storefront.

rules.title: Lens Rules
rules.name: Name
rules.intro: Lens Rules automatically filter which lens options appear based on the customer's prescription. For example: if OD SPH < -4.00, show high-index lens options. Higher priority rules are evaluated first.
rules.noRulesHint: No rules defined yet. Rules let you automatically recommend lens options based on prescription values.

orders.title: Orders
orders.intro: Orders placed through your prescription flows appear here. You can view the prescription details, lens selections, and manage order fulfillment.

flowEditor.title: Flow Editor
flowEditor.selectStep: Select a step from the left to edit it.
flowEditor.selectHint: Each step represents a screen in your customer's prescription journey.
flowEditor.intro: Build your prescription flow step by step.
flowEditor.hintOrder: Steps run top to bottom. Drag arrows to reorder.

tabHint.jumps: Define rules that skip or redirect customers based on their selections.
tabHint.diagram: Visual map of your flow. Click on Logic Jumps to configure conditional navigation.
tabHint.translations: Translate all customer-facing text into additional languages.
tabHint.settings: Flow-level settings: template, animation, cart behavior, and bundle options.

wizard.step1Help: Add steps from the left panel, arrange them in order, then click each step to configure its content on the right.
wizard.step2Help: Optional settings for fine-tuning your flow. Click each section to expand.

pageConfig.title: Title
pageConfig.subtitle: Subtitle
pageConfig.helpTitle: The heading displayed at the top of this step.
pageConfig.helpSubtitle: A secondary heading shown below the title.
pageConfig.helpDescription: Additional context or instruction text for the customer.

prescriptionType.name: Name
prescriptionType.intro: Define the prescription types customers can choose from (e.g. Single Vision, Progressive, Reading). Each type can be linked to specific lens groups so customers only see relevant lenses.
prescriptionType.helpLeadsTo: If set, selecting this option will skip to the chosen node instead of proceeding normally.
prescriptionType.flowHintJump: Buyer will skip to {step} after selecting this prescription
prescriptionType.flowHintJumpDetail: Jump bypasses intermediate steps and takes the buyer directly to the target page
prescriptionType.flowHintNext: Buyer will proceed to the next step in order after selecting this prescription
prescriptionType.flowHintNextDetail: Buyer moves through steps sequentially as defined in the flow
prescriptionType.helpType: Internal identifier for this option. Used by logic jumps and backend matching.
prescriptionType.helpName: Customer-facing label shown on the selection screen.
prescriptionType.helpDescription: Additional text shown to the customer explaining this option.
prescriptionType.helpImageUrl: An image shown alongside this option. Leave empty to show no image.
prescriptionType.helpPrice: Additional price for this prescription type. Set to 0 if no extra cost.
prescriptionType.helpEnabled: Turn off to hide this option without deleting it.
prescriptionType.helpSortOrder: Controls display order. Lower numbers appear first.
prescriptionType.helpLensGroups: Only the selected lens groups will be shown to customers who pick this type. Leave empty to show all available groups.

submitMethod.name: Name
submitMethod.intro: How should customers provide their prescription? Enable Manual (type values), Upload (photo/file), or Fill Later (skip for now). You can also define custom method options that redirect customers to specific steps.
submitMethod.helpJumpTo: Enter a node ref to redirect customers who choose this submission method.

prescriptionForm.helpSPH: Sphere power: corrects nearsightedness (-) or farsightedness (+). Range typically -20 to +20.
prescriptionForm.helpCYL: Cylinder power: corrects astigmatism. Range typically -6 to 0.
prescriptionForm.helpAXIS: Axis angle: orientation of astigmatism correction, 0 to 180 degrees.
prescriptionForm.helpADD: Addition power: extra magnification for reading, typically 0 to +4.
prescriptionForm.helpPD: Pupillary Distance: distance between eye centers, typically 45 to 85 mm.

uploadStep.allowPdSelectorHelp: Let users manually select PD during upload step
uploadStep.helpAcceptTypes: File types customers can upload. Example: image/png,image/jpeg,application/pdf

lensPage.name: Name
lensPage.intro: Configure one or more lens selection pages. Each page displays lens options to the customer. You can control the visual layout and decide whether images, prices, and logic jumps are shown.
lensPage.helpLayout: Grid shows products in rows; List shows vertically; Card adds borders; Compact uses minimal spacing.

lensOption.helpBadge: A small tag shown on the lens option card (e.g. 'Popular', 'Best Value').
lensOption.helpSkip: When enabled, customers can optionally skip this step without selecting a lens.

lensOptionDetail.badgeHelp: Display a badge on this option (e.g. 'Best Value', 'Popular').
lensOptionDetail.title: Title
lensOptionDetail.titleHelp: Custom title. Leave blank to use the bound product title.
lensOptionDetail.descriptionHelp: Description shown in modal (HTML supported). If filled, a question mark icon appears.
lensOptionDetail.noInventoryWarning: This product appears to have no available stock. If "Auto-hide out-of-stock lenses" is enabled in Global Settings, this option will be hidden on the storefront; otherwise it will still be shown.
lensOptionDetail.displayConditionHelp: Show this option only when certain prescription conditions are met.
lensOptionDetail.flowUsageHint: This lens product is referenced by the following flows:
lensOptionDetail.noFlowUsage: This product is not used in any flow yet.
lensOptionDetail.tutorialDescription: How to add a description to an option?
lensOptionDetail.tutorialImage: How to add an image to an option?
lensOptionDetail.tutorialTemplate: How to modify the template styles?

productSelector.title: Select Product & Variant
productSelector.searchError: Search failed. Please check your connection.

productInfoCard.noProduct: No product bound yet. Click 'Change Lens Product' to bind one.

logicJumps.noRules: No jump rules defined. The flow will proceed sequentially through each step.
logicJumps.intro: Logic jumps let you redirect customers to different steps based on their choices or prescription values. Rules are evaluated in order. If a rule matches, the customer is sent to the target step.
logicJumps.helpCondition: Define what triggers this jump. Leave 'From Option' blank to match any option in the step.

reviewOrder.intro: The Order Review step shows customers a summary before checkout. Toggle which sections to display.

settings.helpHideOutOfStockLenses: When enabled, the system checks each lens product's inventory via Shopify in real-time and automatically hides out-of-stock lenses from the storefront.
settings.upgradeDesc: Unlock advanced animations, custom CSS, A/B testing, and priority support.
settings.helpFlowName: Internal name for this flow. Not shown to customers.
settings.helpFlowKey: Unique key used to identify this flow in theme code.
settings.helpTemplate: Layout preset for the storefront widget.
settings.helpAtcBehavior: What happens when a customer adds items to cart.
settings.helpBundleProduct: When enabled, the frame and lens are added as a single bundled product.
settings.helpAnimation: Entry animation applied when the widget appears on the storefront.

publish.liveDesc: Changes will take effect on your storefront after republishing.
publish.draftMessage: This flow is still in draft
publish.draftDesc: Publish it to make it available on your storefront.
publish.instructions: Please confirm the following items are completed:
publish.check1: I have configured the theme app extension
publish.check2: I have added this flow to at least one product
publish.check3: I have tested the flow on my storefront
publish.nonTheme2Desc: If your theme is not an Online Store 2.0 theme, app blocks may not be supported.

contactLensForm.helpBaseCurve: Base curve (BC) values available for contact lenses, measured in mm. Typical range: 8.3 to 9.0.
contactLensForm.helpDiameter: Diameter (DIA) values available, measured in mm. Typical range: 13.8 to 14.5.

textTranslation.selectHint: Select a field key from the left to edit translations.

productAssignment.title: Assigned Products
productAssignment.hint: This flow will appear on the storefront for the assigned products. Search and assign products below.
productAssignment.empty: No products assigned. Search below to add products.
productAssignment.searchPlaceholder: Search products by title or tag...
